goal: Enumerate real scene templates so 10 student pairs each pick a unique term project.
thoughts: Never invent names. list_scene_templates is the single source of truth. Group output by domain (manipulation / navigation / perception) and tag difficulty. Spot-load two or three to confirm they open. If fewer than 10 unique templates exist, say so and suggest parameterized variants (same template, different robot).
code:
# 1. Authoritative enumeration — no hallucination
templates = list_scene_templates()

# 2. Filter by hardware class the students have
filter_templates_by_hardware(gpu_tier="<UNDERGRAD_LAPTOP_TIER>")

# 3. Spot-check two templates actually load
load_scene_template(template_name="<SAMPLE_A>")
load_scene_template(template_name="<SAMPLE_B>")

# 4. Present as handout:
# | Template            | Domain        | Difficulty | Out-of-box motion |
# |---------------------|---------------|------------|-------------------|
# | <TEMPLATE_NAME>     | manipulation  | beginner   | yes (demo policy) |
# ...

# If <10 distinct: suggest robot-swap variants (Franka vs UR10) within same scene
failure_modes: Inventing template names not in the live registry
Dumping raw JSON instead of grouped table
Two pairs pick same template because no uniqueness check
Recommending a template that needs cameras to students with iGPU laptops
